issues.description: USDC is included in ROUTING_TOKENS for mainnet, so the code generates TOKEN→USDC→USDC paths (USDC as both intermediary and destination). These always fail at the quoter (no USDC/USDC pool) and waste 16 RPC calls per price lookup (4×4 fee tier combinations). Should add a check to skip routing_token == usdc.address alongside the existing token == routing_token self-loop check.

Missing test for the core value proposition: a token with a USDT V3 pool but no USDC/WETH V3 pool returning a valid price via the new multi-hop routing. The mission AGENTS.md explicitly lists SIX (0x61C6eBf443AD613c9648762585B3cFD3BA1F3fA8) as a suitable test token for this at block 18000000. The worker tested MIC instead (which returned None) and dropped the test case. The SIX token documented in mission AGENTS.md should have been used.

Path count increased from 8 to 68 per call on mainnet (4 direct + 64 multi-hop). This is 8.5× more RPC calls per price lookup. While igather parallelizes them, this may increase RPC load and latency for archive nodes. The 16 wasteful USDC→USDC paths (from the first issue) exacerbate this. Acceptable for now given the parallelism, but worth monitoring.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS.md documents SIX (0x61C6eBf443AD613c9648762585B3cFD3BA1F3fA8) as having a V3 USDT pool but no USDC/WETH V3 pool at block 18000000, yet the worker used MIC (which failed to return a price) instead of referencing the documented test tokens table. The 'Useful Test Tokens' section was underutilized.

The python-lib-worker skill's 'Finding Test Tokens' section (step 7) says 'Search Etherscan or use on-chain data' but does not mention checking the mission AGENTS.md for pre-documented test tokens first. Adding 'Check mission AGENTS.md for pre-documented test tokens before searching on-chain' would save time and prevent missed test cases.

The skill requires TDD (Write Tests First, Red Phase) but the transcript skeleton shows the worker wrote tests and implementation together, then iterated. The deviation is minor and the final tests are adequate, but the skill procedure was not strictly followed.

summary: PASS. The implementation correctly expands V3 multi-hop routing through all ROUTING_TOKENS as intermediaries. Core logic is sound: ROUTING_TOKENS imported from y.constants, self-loop prevention works (token != routing_token), _encode_path handles all generated path lengths (3 and 5 via _PATH_TYPE_STRINGS), and paths run in parallel via igather. Tests cover WETH regression, USDT/DAI self-loop prevention, graceful None for invalid tokens, and top-level API integration. Manual verification confirmed correct prices for USDT ($1.007), WETH ($1653), and DAI ($1.008). Three non-blocking issues: (1) USDC-as-intermediary creates 16 wasteful TOKEN→USDC→USDC paths per call since USDC is both a routing token and the destination, (2) missing test for the core feature value (token with USDT pool but no USDC/WETH pool — SIX token was documented in mission AGENTS.md but not used), (3) path count grew 8.5× which may increase RPC load.
